classes.name: Aquatics - Parent and Tot Swim
classes.ages: Age at least 6m but less than 3 yrs
classes.desc: This class will teach parents basic water safety while introducing tots to new skills and fun. Songs, water safety, entering and exiting the water, and holding techniques for the parents are some of the topics covered in this lesson. Infant or toddler is accompanied in the water by their caregiver. Children are required to wear swim diapers while in the pool. Swim classes are limited to one class per person per session.

classes.name: Aquatics - Preschool Swim
classes.ages: Age at least 3 yrs but less than 6 yrs
classes.desc: This level continues to focus on building more comfort and enjoyment in the water using songs, games and play. We introduce more structured play in the areas of bobbing, breath control, floating, gliding and kicking on front and back, in addition to arm rotations and other elementary skills. Children ages 3 and under are required to wear swim diapers while in the pool. The ratio is 1 adult per child. Swim classes are limited to one class per person per session.

classes.name: Aquatics - Learn to Swim - Balboa Pool
classes.when: Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 10:30 AM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 18 yrs
classes.desc: Level 1 This class is designed to orient participants to the aquatic environment and to help them gain basic aquatic skills. In addition, participants start learning about how to be safe around water. Children must be 48" to participate. Level 2 This class builds on the basic aquatic skills and water safety skills and concepts learned in level 1. Participants begin to perform skills at a slightly more advanced level and begin gaining rudimentary propulsive skills on both the front and the back. This level marks the beginning of independent aquatic locomotion skills. Prerequisite- Completion of Level 1. Children must be 48" to participate. Level 3 This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48" to participate. Level 4 This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3. Level 5 This class focuses on helping participants refine their performance of all six swimming strokes (front crawl, back crawl, butterfly, breaststroke, elementary backstroke, and sidestroke). Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. Participants also learn to perform flip turns on the front and back. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 4.Registration is limited to two sessions per person.

classes.name: Kayaking at India Basin
classes.desc: Discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Paddle at your own pace as you explore the lake’s tranquil waters, diverse wildlife, and natural habitats—an ideal way to connect with nature and enjoy a refreshing outdoor adventure right in the city. Enrollment includes the rental of a tandem (two-person) kayak and covers up to two participants. Personal flotation devices are provided for all participants. Children 12 and under must be accompanied by an adult in the kayak. All participants must sign a release waiver on the day of their session. Instructional safety videos must be viewed prior to paddling; a link to the videos and waiver will be emailed the week before your scheduled time. Registered participants under the age of 12 must be accompanied on the water by a registered parent or guardian. Expect to get wet—please bring a towel and a change of clothes.

classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 10 yrs
classes.desc: Ring in the holiday season with this festive and creative workshop where kids and caregivers work together to create one-of-a-kind ornaments, eco friendly wreaths, and paper bag stockings using paint and recycled materials to add a personal touch creating lasting holiday keepsakes. Spaces are limited. If you have more than 1 child participating, you must enroll for 2 spaces.

classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 10 yrs
classes.desc: Join us for a fun and creative puppet-making workshop where you and your children can express your imagination and create a unique, movable puppet using a variety of materials, including fabric, felt, paper, and recycled goods. Spaces are limited. If you have more than 1 child participating, you must enroll for 2 spaces.

classes.name: Thanksgiving Family Workshop
classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 10 yrs
classes.desc: Join us for a fun and festive Thanksgiving workshop where you and your kids can unleash your creativity, collaborate, and craft unique decorations. Make paper turkeys, air dry clay candle holders, and charming beaded garlands to create holiday-keepsakes and add a personal touch to your Thanksgiving decor. Spaces are limited. If you have more than 1 child participating, you must enroll for 2 spaces.

classes.name: Mini Cinema
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Got a favorite movie? Ever wonder how the great movies got their magic? This class teaches you how! This class is an exploration of cinema style and technique through replication, imitation, creation of cinema, movies, TV, and net series. The class watches and analyzes famous movies, focusing on specific techniques, which they then replicate in class. First we watch it, then we do it! This class will use short scenes, script analysis, making short-films, lighting, camera drills, and students will practice acting and blocking tactics for movie success.

classes.desc: Work on projects using sketches, photos and still life. Please bring your own art materials: paper, canvas, charcoal, acrylics, and watercolors. No oil base product paints. No instructor in this class, class is organized by volunteer coordinators.

classes.name: Beaded Light - Catchers and Ornaments
classes.desc: Craft your own beaded light-catchers and ornaments using lightweight acrylic beads and wire or monofilament. Your 2-D and 3-D creations will sparkle cheerfully. Learn several beading techniques from simple to more complex as we make all kinds of creatures and designs. Step by step instructions with graphics in addition to teacher support guarantee with a fun time and learning success.

classes.name: Beaded Light - Catchers and Ornaments
classes.desc: NOTE: END DATE FOR THIS ACTIVITY HAS CHANGED Craft your own beaded light-catchers and ornaments using lightweight acrylic beads and wire or monofilament. Your 2-D and 3-D creations will sparkle cheerfully. Learn several beading techniques from simple to more complex as we make all kinds of creatures and designs. Step by step instructions with graphics in addition to teacher support guarantee with a fun time and learning success.

classes.name: Chinese Brush Painting
classes.desc: Learn the fundamentals of Chinese brush painting strokes taught by our senior volunteer with many years of experience. Learn the basic techniques of Chinese painting. Participants must purchase their own art supplies. Class meets twice a month on the first and third Tuesday of each month. (1st and 3rd Tuesday of month)

classes.name: Fun with Paper
classes.desc: In this class we will be making paper projects like greeting cards, boxes, books, table favors and gifts. You will take home a completed project at the conclusion of each meeting. Required supplies are ruler, pen, pencil, scissors, glue and tape. Also useful but not required are dies, stamps and inks for stamping. For most projects, paper will be supplied.

classes.name: Jewelry Making (Intermediate Level)
classes.desc: This is an intermediate Jewelry class. This class is based on off loom bead weaving. Project oriented with projects selected by class participants. Participants MUST know the basics of bead weaving and have fundamental skills of off loom work. Instructor will provide assistance and advance your knowledge of techniques.

classes.name: Knitting and Crocheting
classes.desc: Share your skills and techniques with each other in this volunteer-led program. Participants must bring their own supplies. Come share ideas about knitting and crocheting.

classes.name: Life Painting (Intermediate & Model)
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level activity focused on drawing and painting the human head and body. There is no instructor, so it's a great opportunity for self-guided practice and peer learning. We may work from live, fully clothed volunteer models including members of the group who are willing to model or from photos, reproductions, or other reference images that you bring. Please bring your own art materials, including a portable easel if needed. You are welcome to work in any medium, as long as it does not have a strong odor. Come enjoy a relaxed, collaborative environment where you can learn from fellow artists, practice your technique, and try your hand at modeling, if you're open to it!

classes.name: Sketching and Painting for Seniors
classes.desc: This class will focus on sketching and painting through various mediums, including pencils, watercolors, and more. There will be a sketching component every week, as well as ongoing personal projects.

classes.name: Basic Sewing Workshop
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Join us for a hands-on introduction to sewing in this beginner-friendly class. You'll learn essential sewing machine skills including how to thread a machine, wind bobbins, cut fabric, and troubleshoot basic issues. Practice key techniques like straight stitching, box corners, hemming, and using a simple pattern. All materials provided.

classes.name: Basic Sewing Workshop
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Join us for a hands-on introduction to sewing in this beginner-friendly class. You'll learn essential sewing machine skills including how to thread a machine, wind bobbins, cut fabric, and troubleshoot basic issues. Practice key techniques like straight stitching, box corners, hemming, and using a simple pattern. All materials provided.

classes.name: Cartooning and Graphic Novels for Adults
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Students will create their own characters and storylines. By the end of the class, they will have a mini comic book created. Class will cover character expression, body movement, backgrounds in perspective, color and story structure. Basic materials will be provided for students to use during class.

classes.name: Clay Creations: Polymer Bake Clay Workshop
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 11 yrs
classes.desc: Discover the colorful world of polymer clay in this fun, hands-on one-day workshop designed especially for youth makers and young artists! Participants will learn the basics of working with polymer clay, including shaping, blending colors, adding texture, and creating miniature sculptures, charms, jewelry, or imaginative keepsakes. Through guided projects and creative exploration, students will develop fine motor skills, artistic confidence, and their own unique style. No prior experience is needed, just creativity and curiosity! All materials are provided, and students will leave with several pieces and instructions to take home, bake and enjoy.

classes.name: Holiday Workshop: Ornaments
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Ring in the holiday season with this festive and creative workshop where you will create one-of-a-kind ornaments and more in a relaxed and inspiring atmosphere. This workshop offers plenty of creative freedom for all skill levels. No experience is necessary, and all materials are provided. Participants will leave with several completed holiday pieces and inspiration to continue creating at home.

classes.name: Holiday Workshop: Youth Polymer Bake Clay Workshop
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: Celebrate the holiday season with this festive, hands-on polymer clay workshop designed especially for youth makers and young artists! Participants will learn the basics of working with polymer clay, including shaping, blending colors, adding texture, and creating holiday-themed miniatures, ornaments, charms, jewelry, and seasonal keepsakes. Through guided projects and creative exploration, students will build fine motor skills, artistic confidence, and their own unique style while crafting cheerful creations inspired by the holidays. No prior experience is needed—just creativity and holiday spirit! All materials are provided, and students will leave with several pieces and instructions to take home, bake and enjoy.

classes.name: Marbling Workshop
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Discover the mesmerizing art of marbling in this hands-on workshop. Participants will explore traditional and contemporary marbling techniques, learning how to create flowing, one-of-a-kind patterns on paper and fabric using paints and specialized tools. Guided step by step, you’ll experiment with color, movement, and composition while gaining an understanding of the materials and processes behind this centuries-old craft. No prior experience is necessary, just curiosity and a willingness to play. The workshop offers a relaxed, creative environment where you can slow down, enjoy the meditative rhythm of marbling, and leave with several finished pieces to take home. Perfect for artists, crafters, or anyone looking for a unique and inspiring creative experience.

classes.name: Background Design for Comics & Animation
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Students will learn how to create dynamic backgrounds using one and two point perspective in both interior and exterior settings, as well as imaginative dreamscape environments. The course will cover shading, color theory, texture, lighting, and other techniques that enhance storyboards and finished artwork. Students will work with both traditional materials on paper and digital tools on the iPad, developing skills across multiple mediums.

classes.name: Character Design for Comics & Animation
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.desc: Students will develop characters using traditional drawing tools on paper as well as digital tools on iPads, focusing on expression, body movement, and perspective. The course will cover gesture drawing, color theory, and production art techniques that support visual storytelling. Students will also explore storyboarding and background design.

classes.name: Mini Cinema
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Got a favorite movie? Ever wonder how the great movies got their magic? This class teaches you how! This class is an exploration of cinema style and technique through replication, imitation, creation of cinema, movies, TV, and net series. The class watches and analyzes famous movies, focusing on specific techniques, which they then replicate in class. First we watch it, then we do it! This class will use short scenes, script analysis, making short-films, lighting, camera drills, and students will practice acting and blocking tactics for movie success.

classes.name: Adobe Lightroom for Photographers
classes.desc: This four-session course is designed for adult photographers who want to learn how to efficiently organize, edit, and enhance their images using Adobe Lightroom. Whether you’re new to Lightroom or looking to strengthen your workflow, this hands-on class will guide you through the essential tools photographers use to manage large photo libraries and produce polished, professional-looking images. Over four 2-hour sessions, participants will learn how to import and organize photos, use Lightroom’s powerful non-destructive editing tools, and develop a consistent editing style. The course will also cover techniques for color correction, exposure adjustments, cropping, local edits, presets, and preparing images for printing or sharing online. Two 8X10 prints are included.

classes.name: Basic Black and White Film Development
classes.desc: Learn the fundamentals of black-and-white film development in this introductory workshop. Participants will be guided through the process of properly developing black-and-white film, with instruction covering both 35mm and 120 film formats. This class is ideal for beginners and requires no prior experience.Students must bring one exposed roll of black-and-white film ready for processing. Please note that color film development will not be covered, and no class time will be spent working in the darkroom. Completion of a Photo Center Orientation is required for anyone wishing to use the darkroom facilities after the workshop.

classes.name: Basic Black and White Film Development
classes.desc: Learn the fundamentals of black-and-white film development in this introductory workshop. Participants will be guided through the process of properly developing black-and-white film, with instruction covering both 35mm and 120 film formats. This class is ideal for beginners and requires no prior experience.Students must bring one exposed roll of black-and-white film ready for processing. Please note that color film development will not be covered, and no class time will be spent working in the darkroom. Completion of a Photo Center Orientation is required for anyone wishing to use the darkroom facilities after the workshop.

classes.name: Basic Black and White Film Development
classes.desc: Learn the fundamentals of black-and-white film development in this introductory workshop. Participants will be guided through the process of properly developing black-and-white film, with instruction covering both 35mm and 120 film formats. This class is ideal for beginners and requires no prior experience.Students must bring one exposed roll of black-and-white film ready for processing. Please note that color film development will not be covered, and no class time will be spent working in the darkroom. Completion of a Photo Center Orientation is required for anyone wishing to use the darkroom facilities after the workshop.

classes.name: Basic Black and White Film Development
classes.desc: Learn the fundamentals of black-and-white film development in this introductory workshop. Participants will be guided through the process of properly developing black-and-white film, with instruction covering both 35mm and 120 film formats. This class is ideal for beginners and requires no prior experience.Students must bring one exposed roll of black-and-white film ready for processing. Please note that color film development will not be covered, and no class time will be spent working in the darkroom. Completion of a Photo Center Orientation is required for anyone wishing to use the darkroom facilities after the workshop.

classes.name: Basic Black and White Film Development
classes.desc: Learn the basics of black and white film development. In this quick workshop students will be taught how to properly process their black and white film. 35mm and 120 film development will all be explained. Students must bring in a black and white roll of film ready for development. There are no prerequisites and beginners are encouraged to sign-up. No class time will be spent in the darkroom and color film development will not be addressed in this course. Photo Center Orientation is required for darkroom.

classes.name: Cyanotype for All
classes.desc: Join us for a one-day workshop exploring one of the oldest photographic processes, the cyanotype. This alternative process produces a characteristic blue hued photographic print. In this workshop participants will use found objects like flowers, plants, or other common items to create unique photograms using the cyanotype process. We will cover the basics of the process from mixing and applying emulsion to exposure and development. Participants are encouraged to bring their own objects with them on the day. Advice will be provided on suitable objects.

classes.name: Darkroom Essentials & Processes
classes.desc: This introductory course covers the fundamentals of black-and-white darkroom printing. Participants will learn the essential techniques and processes needed to transform negatives into finished prints, including enlarger operation, exposure control, and basic print development. Through hands-on instruction and practice, students will build the skills and confidence needed to continue exploring traditional darkroom photography.The instructor will review required materials during the first class. This course may be taken in lieu of the Photography Center Orientation requirement.

classes.name: Darkroom Essentials & Processes
classes.desc: This introductory course covers the fundamentals of black-and-white darkroom printing. Participants will learn the essential techniques and processes needed to transform negatives into finished prints, including enlarger operation, exposure control, and basic print development. Through hands-on instruction and practice, students will build the skills and confidence needed to continue exploring traditional darkroom photography.The instructor will review required materials during the first class. This course may be taken in lieu of the Photography Center Orientation requirement.

classes.name: Developing Your Personal Vision
classes.desc: This hands-on class is designed for the intermediate photographer who wants to take their technical proficiency to the next level exploring concept, subject matter and self-expression. Emphasis is placed on developing a personal vision and approach to picture making. The class work can be done in any format: digital, film, black & white, or color. Solid basic photographic knowledge is required. The instructor will lead insightful, engaging critiques of student work, assign a theme and give individual feedback on the progress of the work. A brief history of photography will be presented and the work of noted photographers will be introduced.

classes.name: Digital Collage with Photoshop
classes.desc: Bring your artistic vision to life! This workshop focuses on composition, effective use of layers, masks, filters and blending tools to create exciting and evocative layered imagery in Photoshop. We’ll play with scanned textures and objects, personal photographs to create a narrative. Some familiarity with Adobe Photoshop is recommended but not required.

classes.name: Art Expression for Seniors
classes.desc: Discover and develop your own unique style of creative expression. We’ll explore a variety of art forms and experiment with materials from painting to collage to mixed media. Participants are also encouraged to bring their own projects to share with the group and receive feedback and ideas. No previous art experience necessary.

classes.name: Community Sewing Class - Beginners
classes.desc: This course introduces students to the fundamentals of sewing and is designed for beginners. Participants will learn to operate industrial sewing machinery while gaining practical experience in garment construction. Weekly lessons will focus on essential techniques, including installing zippers, attaching collars, adding pockets, performing basic alterations, and assembling garments. By the end of the course, students will have developed the skills necessary to complete a variety of sewing projects with confidence.

classes.name: Arts and Crafts
classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 9 yrs
classes.desc: This class is a fun and creative space where you can explore and express yourself through painting, sculpture, and much more!

classes.name: Beginning Coding for Minecraft
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Dive into the exciting world of coding by creating your own games and interactive adventures with Minecraft! Participants will learn coding concepts through hands-on projects, using code to customize gameplay, build unique features, and bring their ideas to life. Students can collaborate with classmates to solve challenges, create new experiences, and test their creations in both cooperative and friendly competitive activities. Along the way, participants will develop valuable skills such as problem-solving, creativity, teamwork, and digital literacy, while having fun coding and playing in the Minecraft universe. Tablets are available for use during class, or students may bring their own device with the Minecraft app already purchased and installed.

classes.name: Beginning Coding for Minecraft
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Dive into the exciting world of coding by creating your own games and interactive adventures with Minecraft! Participants will learn coding concepts through hands-on projects, using code to customize gameplay, build unique features, and bring their ideas to life. Students can collaborate with classmates to solve challenges, create new experiences, and test their creations in both cooperative and friendly competitive activities. Along the way, participants will develop valuable skills such as problem-solving, creativity, teamwork, and digital literacy, while having fun coding and playing in the Minecraft universe. Tablets are available for use during class, or students may bring their own device with the Minecraft app already purchased and installed.

classes.name: Audio Production
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Explore the exciting world of audio production in this beginner-friendly class for youth! Participants will learn the fundamentals of creating and recording sound, including music production, sound effects, voice recording for podcasts, radio, and film, and basic audio editing techniques. Using microphones, keyboards, digital sequencers, and audio production software, students will create their own electronic music projects, record original sounds and dialogue, and learn the basics of mixing and sound design.Whether you're interested in making music, or creating movie-style sound effects, this hands-on class offers a fun and creative introduction to audio production. From GarageBand to Star Wars-inspired sound design, come make some noise—and learn how to capture it!

classes.name: Mini Cinema
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Got a favorite movie? Ever wonder how the great movies got their magic? This class teaches you how! This class is an exploration of cinema style and technique through replication, imitation, creation of cinema, movies, TV, and net series. The class watches and analyzes famous movies, focusing on specific techniques, which they then replicate in class. First we watch it, then we do it! This class will use short scenes, script analysis, making short-films, lighting, camera drills, and students will practice acting and blocking tactics for movie success.

classes.name: Architects for the Tiny
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Have fun imagining and creating any interior space in miniature: A cat cafe, your dream clubhouse, or maybe your future home! Try your hand at interior decorating as you build furniture, select textiles and create scale models with our ample supply of tools, craft supplies and repurposed items.

classes.name: Art and Animals
classes.ages: Age at least 2y 6m but less than 6 yrs
classes.desc: Each week, a different animal is the star of our classroom. We learn fun facts and explore the wonders of these amazing creatures. Often, a live animal pays us a visit in person; other times we closely examine skeletons, taxidermy models, and habitats. Then, we get busy making art inspired by their unique characteristics. Projects, techniques and animal vary every session, but you can count on having fun, getting messy and learning something new every week. This hands-on class is as fun for the accompanying adults as it is for the kids. Class fee includes one child and one participating adult. Please register using the child's name.

classes.name: Artist Workshop
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 11 yrs
classes.desc: Get busy creating as we learn about, explore and practice with the exciting variety of mediums and tools in the Randall Museum's Art Studio. We'll workshop projects, techniques and creative approaches in every class and see where the process leads us!

classes.name: Beginning/ Intermediate Knitting for Youth
classes.ages: Age at least 11 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Whether you want to learn the basics or are ready to take your skills to the next level, this class is a supportive and welcoming space where young crafters can gather with other knitting enthusiasts. Beginners will learn to knit, purl, cast on and cast off. Intermediate students will learn new techniques and get support with their projects. Yarn and needles are provided for practice and the instructor will provide a wide variety of patterns. Once you have chosen a project, you’ll be expected to bring your own yarn and knitting needles to class.

classes.name: Beginning/Intermediate Knitting
classes.desc: Hats? Scarves? Cowls? Blankets? Fingerless Gloves? Socks? In this class designed for both beginning and intermediate knitters, anything is possible! Beginners will learn to knit, purl, cast on and cast off. Intermediate knitters will learn new techniques. Yarn and needles are provided for practice, and the instructor will provide a variety of knitting patterns. Once you've chosen your project, you'll be expected to bring your own yarn and knitting needles to class.

classes.name: New Technologies: 3D Printing
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 10 yrs
classes.desc: Explore the awesome world of 3D printing and modeling! You'll jump right in designing and building your own simple three-dimensional objects in TinkerCad, a 3D modeling program. Then you’ll export your files to our MakerBot Replicator 3D printers, and learn how to finish & refine the printed objects. This is a project-based class and consistent attendance is required, those who miss the first 2 classes or who are excessively late or absent may be dropped without refund.

classes.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: The Collaborative Mosaic
classes.desc: Join us for a high energy holiday art workshop focused on creating a Collaborative Mosaic Abstract Acrylic Painting! This workshop combines the joy of a party with a deep dive into professional art techniques. Each artist starts with a 12x12 inch wood panel, painting for ten minutes before passing it to the neighbor on their right. The rotation continues until every panel has traveled the entire circle, ensuring every hand contributes to each piece. To elevate our collaborative work, for a variety of effects we will experiment with an extensive library of acrylic mediums, including gesso, molding paste, heavy gel, soft gel, tar gel, glazing medium, pouring medium, crackle paste, matte medium, and UV-reactive mediums. Once the panels complete their journey, we will connect them to reveal one monumental abstract mosaic. As a group, we will rotate and rearrange the individual panels, exploring the endless visual possibilities and stories hidden within our collective masterpiece. Come celebrate life and art as we build something beautiful together! Please bring your own brushes, palette knives, and a soap to clean the brushes. Acrylic paints, wood panels, and acrylic mediums provided. Light refreshments will also be provided. Painting is thirsty work!

classes.name: Abstract Printmaking: Texture, Shape & Color
classes.desc: This class is a playful, low-pressure dive into abstract image-making through hand printed techniques. We will focus on core visual principles: texture, repetition, color, and composition while creating intuitive, expressive prints using linoleum blocks, gelatin plates, and collage. With zero pressure to draw something or create representational images, students will build visual fundamentals organically, making it a great fit for beginners or rusty creatives. Emphasizing process, intuition, and play, this class encourages flow-state work that’s restorative and creatively satisfying. Each student will develop a mini-series of prints or a visual journal exploring their own personal visual language. Please bring required materials to class. Materials list available at sharonartstudio.org.

classes.name: Advanced Glass
classes.desc: This is an advanced class. In order to enroll, students must have 6 months of stained glass experience to register and may not be enrolled in another Leaded Glass class. Glass fusing allowed during this class. Firing will be done by the instructor in communal kiln. Class is self-directed, minimal instruction provided.

classes.name: Bezel Setting with Cabochons & Independent Study
classes.desc: In this beginner class, each student can expect to design and fabricate a unique sterling silver stone set ring. Students will learn foundational silversmithing techniques- cut metal with the jewelers saw, sand, file, anneal, stamp, form, solder, create a bezel cup, patina and polish in order to fabricate their final product. This class is a great opportunity to learn a new skill or brush up on existing metalsmithing skills. All levels of experience are welcome. Materials and tools are provided with the cost of the class, but you are welcome to bring your own cabochons and supplies. If this will be your first ring and you would like to purchase your own cabochons to use in the class, please buy oval or circular. The Jewelry Studio is located on the upper floor, with stair access only. For accommodations requests or accessibility questions, please contact rpdaccess@sfgov.org or 415-831-6841. Please note that submitting your request at least two weeks before the beginning of the class will help ensure availability.

classes.name: Block Printing in Layers
classes.desc: Bring your block prints to life with color and shape. Design and carve multiple blocks to create an edition of multi-layered color prints. The instructor will cover color separations, registration, and ink mixing for clean and colorful prints. This class is open to all levels. Please bring required materials to class. Materials list available at sharonartstudio.org.

classes.name: Casting in Bronze
classes.desc: Learn the organic metal art of lost wax casting. This course will cover the process by which metal object is cast from a wax model, allowing you to see the limitless forms that metal can take. The class starts by showing you how to work with wax (hot and cold) to create pieces of jewelry that will be cast. The class fee covers the cost of basic materials (including bronze). Each student will be given one 2 1/2 inch flask to fit their wax designs in. Students who wish to work in silver are responsible for purchasing sterling silver which will be covered in class. Instructor will be doing the castings for the students. Beginners are welcome. The Jewelry Studio is located on the upper floor, with stair access only. For accommodations requests or accessibility questions, please contact rpdaccess@sfgov.org or 415-831-6841. Please note that submitting your request at least two weeks before the beginning of the class will help ensure availability.

classes.name: Casting in Bronze
classes.desc: Learn the organic metal art of lost wax casting. This course will cover the process by which metal object is cast from a wax model, allowing you to see the limitless forms that metal can take. The class starts by showing you how to work with wax (hot and cold) to create pieces of jewelry that will be cast. The class fee covers the cost of basic materials (including bronze). Each student will be given one 2 1/2 inch flask to fit their wax designs in. Students who wish to work in silver are responsible for purchasing sterling silver which will be covered in class. Instructor will be doing the castings for the students. Beginners are welcome. The Jewelry Studio is located on the upper floor, with stair access only. For accommodations requests or accessibility questions, please contact rpdaccess@sfgov.org or 415-831-6841. Please note that submitting your request at least two weeks before the beginning of the class will help ensure availability.

classes.name: Camp Mather Senior Getaway Lottery Registration
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Fri · 10:00 PM - 5:05 PM on the next day
classes.desc: **Enter the lottery for the Senior Getaway by clicking Enroll button and answering questions. Then continue to check-out to receive your enrollment receipt. Results will be available in early June** I've updated the description to better address common camper questions: Senior Camp Mather Getaway (August 16–20) The Senior Camp Mather Getaway is a four-night adventure filled with relaxation, fresh air, and time to enjoy nature. Guests will stay in cozy cabins equipped with beds, dressers, and electricity. Each cabin accommodates 2–6 people. Individuals registering independently will be paired with a roommate. Well-lit bathhouses with toilets and showers are located near the cabins. Cafeteria style meals are provided throughout the stay. A variety of optional activities are available, including arts and crafts, sing-alongs, exercise classes, nature hikes, a one day trip to Yosemite National Park, and daily programming. This is truly a getaway experience. Cost for the getaway: $425 Click here for more information.

classes.name: Kezar Baseball Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: tep up to the plate and join us for a week of all-things baseball! Designed for both newcomers and experienced players, this camp focuses on building fundamental skills, enhancing game knowledge, and fostering confidence on the field. Through action-packed drills, team-building activities, and plenty of game time, campers will sharpen their abilities in hitting, throwing, catching, and base running while deepening their understanding of how the game is played.Our experienced coaches create a fun, supportive environment where players of all levels can grow their skills, build teamwork, and develop a passion for the sport. While the focus is strictly on baseball, there will be some limited time for supervised free play to keep things balanced.Whether your camper is learning the ropes or refining their game, this camp is perfect for growing as a player and teammate. Let’s play ball!

classes.name: Archery & Adventure Winter Camp
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 14 yrs
classes.desc: Archery & Adventure Camp is a unique and inclusive camp at the oldest archery range in the country! Your child will learn basic archery skills using recurve and compound bows along with all necessary shooting and safety equipment provided. USA Archery certified instructors will provide the proper techniques, help build hand/eye coordination, and self-confidence in a fun and safe environment. Other activities include various field sports games, archery-themed arts and crafts projects, offsite field trips, and Archery Tag on the last day at Ocean Beach! Drop off & pick up is at Cabrillo Playground.

classes.name: Camp Mather Lottery Registration
classes.when: Sat · 11:59 PM - 10:59 PM on the next day
classes.desc: ONE LOTTERY REGISTRATION PER HOUSEHOLD There is a $50 lottery application fee. If you receive a reservation, this will be applied to your invoice. If you cancel, you will forfeit the $50. If you do not receive a reservation, the $50 will be credited to your Rec and Park account. This fee is not refundable if you cancel your reservation. Read the Registration and Reservation Information before applying. SF Residents: Click here to upload your proof of residency documents. In order to be included in the SF Resident Lottery, proof of residency will be required with registration and due by 1/2/26 at 5pm. Lottery applications still close on 1/2/26 at 5pm. Camp Mather is a rustic family camp located right next door to Yosemite. With full recreation amenities including a swimming pool, swimming lake, sport activities, supervised recreation programs for all ages, evening campfire entertainment and more, you and your family will enjoy this week-long vacation. Stay in one of our rustic cabins or reserve a tent site. Guests enjoy 3 delicious meals each day, served cafeteria style with excellent service. Please read all registration and reservation information prior to registering into the lottery. Go to the Camp Mather webpage for all info on Camp Mather, including registration and reservation and Campership information. June 21-27 is Inclusion Week. Open to all! During this week, Recreation and Parks will provide trained staff to help facilitate participation for visitors with disabilities in camp activities, and provide some great specialized activities. We invite you to register for any week at camp, but we encourage you to consider choosing Inclusion Week. We are confident the enhanced activities will benefit all with and without disabilities, as well as offer all the children the opportunity to make new friends. Lottery Registration closes at 5pm on Friday 1/2/26.

classes.name: Randall Museum Overnight Youth Camp
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Come with us to Camp Mather, San Francisco's family camp near Yosemite National Park. Discover the wonders of the Sierra Nevada mountains with nature exploration, hikes, swimming, games, art projects and evening campfires! We will travel from the Randall Museum in vans and spend 5 days and 4 nights tent camping under the evergreens and the stars. While we're at Camp Mather, we'll eat in the cafeteria and swim in the pool and the lake under the watchful eyes of Mather lifeguards. We'll take hikes and explore nearby natural wonders like the pristine Hetch Hetchy Reservoir where San Francisco's drinking water comes from. MANDATORY pre-camp meeting.

classes.name: Crocker Amazon - Summer Camp- Ages 6-11
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 5y 9m but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: Get ready for an unforgettable summer at the Crocker-Amazon Sports Camp! Each week, campers will dive into a dynamic lineup of baseball, basketball, flag football, soccer, and tennis. Led by our passionate and experienced coaches, every child will build rock-solid fundamentals, sharpen their skills, and learn how to compete in a fun, supportive, team-focused environment. Whether they’re trying a new sport or leveling up in a favorite one, our goal is to spark a love for athletics that lasts long after summer ends—into the school year and for many years to come!

classes.name: Wetlands Explorer - Summer Camp Ages 6-11
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: Wetland Explorers Summer Camp is located at the EcoCenter, at Heron’s Head Park and serves youth ages 6-11 years old for two-week sessions. Featuring outdoor play areas, beach-access, and an abundance of wildlife, the EcoCenter is the perfect place for discovering the wetlands of the San Francisco Bay. Our program is filled with outdoor exploration, science learning, nature crafts, exciting field trips, and more! This camp does not offer extended care.

classes.name: Great Time Out Winter Break Camp
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 14 yrs
classes.desc: It’s a great time to have a ball! Great Timeout Camp offers kids the chance to learn and play a variety of sports, try indoor rock climbing, and enjoy the scenic outdoors of Glen Canyon Park. With a focus on teamwork and sportsmanship, campers will create lasting friendships and memories. Equipment for rock climbing is provided.

classes.name: Silver Tree - Summer Camp- Ages 6-11
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: A San Francisco tradition, Silver Tree Day Camp is located in Glen Park Canyon. Silver Tree is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, campfire safety, arts and crafts, legends storytelling and rock climbing. Camp hours are from 9am-3:30pm, except for Thursdays. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s'mores. Extended care hours are included in the camp fee from 8:30am-9:00am for drop off. Pick up is no later than 5:30pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building every day for pick up (about a 10 minute walk into the Canyon).

classes.name: Silver Tree LIT - Summer Camp- Ages 12-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 12 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: The Silver Tree Leaders In Training (L.I.T.) is located in Glen Canyon Park. Participants must be between the ages of 12 to 14 years old. The LIT program is a 2-week camp experience introducing young teens to leadership, assisting Silver Tree staff with camper mentorship, and developing teamwork skills in the hopes of preparing LITs to become staff in the summer camp setting. LITs will also participate in a variety of team building exercises, campfire building, basic wilderness safety, rock climbing, and will be lightly incorporated into traditional Silver Tree activities. Camp hours are from 9am-4pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building for the first day of camp, which is a 10-minute walk into Glen Canyon. Registration for this camp is limited to one session per person.

classes.name: Camp In, Climb On Overnight
classes.when: Sun & Sat · 6:00 PM - 10:00 AM on the next day
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Calling all young explorers! Join us for a night of outdoor fun at Glen Park Rec Center. This exciting overnight adventure will teach camping basics like pitching a tent (with tents provided), cooking a camping-style dinner, and sleeping under the stars (indoors, of course!). We’ll also hit the trails for a canyon hike and try some indoor rock climbing with all gear provided. Our certified staff will lead the way, ensuring a safe and fun experience. Dinner and breakfast are provided. Just bring your adventure spirit!

classes.name: Winter Wonderland Camp
classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: Join us for Winter Wonderland Camp during holiday break! Campers will enjoy a fun-filled winter adventure with swimming, arts and crafts, field trips, sports, games, and engaging activities. Children will make new friends, stay active, explore their creativity, and create lasting memories while celebrating the magic of the winter season. Each day brings new opportunities for fun, learning, and adventure in a safe and supportive environment. Don't miss out on a wonderful winter break experience!

classes.name: YPTMTC Performance & Creative Art - Summer Camp - Ages 13-17
classes.location: Harvey Milk Center For The Arts
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · Noon - 5:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 13 yrs but less than 18 yrs
classes.desc: Join us for a two-week creative arts intensive where teens choose one of three tracks: Musical Theatre, Vocal & Acting, or Creative Writing. Each program offers daily training led by experienced professionals, building skills in performance, storytelling, technique, and collaboration. Whether you're singing, acting, or writing, you’ll develop your craft while working toward a final showcase featuring songs, scenes, or staged readings. Full attendance and participation in the final showcase are required. Partial attendance is not permitted. This camp does not offer extended care. For questions, contact Nicola Bosco-Alvarez at director@yptmtc.org.

classes.name: MM-LAFTS Tennis - Summer Camp Ages 9-15
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 9 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: The Maunupau Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture, drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, & western(full), stances; square, closed, & open, the difference between topspin & underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play, & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision making process. Campers are never under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves with sportspersonship on court.

classes.name: Kezar Basketball Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: The Kezar Basketball Camp helps athletes develop a solid foundation in basketball fundamentals while deepening their knowledge of the game. Each day includes engaging drills, team-building activities, and scrimmages that promote confidence and on-court growth. Our upbeat, knowledgeable coaching staff keeps the atmosphere fun and supportive as players progress throughout the summer. This camp is designed for all experience levels—from first-time hoopers to dedicated players. Please wear athletic clothing and closed-toed athletic shoes.

classes.name: Kezar Flag Football Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: At the Kezar Flag Football Camp, players dive into the core fundamentals of the sport while building a strong understanding of game strategy and teamwork. Campers take part in energetic drills, skill-focused games, and exciting scrimmages designed to boost confidence and ability. With an emphasis on fun, development, and guidance from our enthusiastic, experienced coaches, participants sharpen their skills all summer long. Whether you’re brand new to football or looking to elevate your game, this camp welcomes all levels.

classes.name: Kezar Girls Track & Field Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs

classes.name: Kezar Racquet Sports - Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Racquet Sports Camp gives campers the chance to try three exciting sports—tennis, pickleball, and ping pong. Through daily rotations, campers develop coordination, teamwork, and confidence while enjoying an active and fun summer experience

classes.name: Kezar Track & Field Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: An introduction to track and field events, this program teaches training techniques for sprints, middle-distance, and long-distance runs, as well as high jump and long jump.

classes.name: Kezar Volleyball Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: The Kezar Volleyball Camp invites players to develop strong fundamentals and a deeper grasp of the game through engaging instruction and hands-on practice. Campers participate in fun drills, teamwork-focused activities, and scrimmages that build confidence and skill. Guided by our enthusiastic, knowledgeable volleyball coaches, athletes make meaningful progress as the summer unfolds. Suitable for beginners and experienced players alike, this camp offers a supportive and exciting environment for all.

classes.name: Kezar Soccer Summer Camp - Ages 6-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: At the Kezar Soccer Camp, players build and refine their understanding of essential soccer skills through dynamic instruction and game-based learning. Campers enjoy a mix of drills, team activities, and spirited scrimmages that foster confidence, collaboration, and love for the sport. With the support of our energetic, experienced coaches, athletes see steady improvement throughout the summer. From newcomers to seasoned players, everyone is welcome on the pitch.

classes.name: Camp Sukahica - Summer Camp- Ages 10-13
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 14 yrs
classes.desc: Explore the wild beauty of Northern California’s coast through hiking, kayaking, and surfing. SUKAHICA is an outdoor adventure camp for kids seeking fun, challenge, and a deeper connection to nature. Campers kayak on Lake Merced and surf at local beaches, with lessons tailored for all levels—from first waves to skill building. It’s more than just a camp—it’s about creating lasting memories and friendships in the great outdoors. Swim Test Required: Email rex.biteng@sfgov.org? or download the form from our website. Bring completed form on day one. Test includes: Tread water for 1 minute, Swim 75 yards (crawl or breaststroke) Must be done at a pool with certified lifeguards. Cancellations due to test failure follow the camp withdrawal policy.

classes.name: Pathway LIT - Summer Camp- Ages 13-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 13 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: L.I.T Pathways is a dynamic two-week leadership program designed for motivated 13–14-year-olds ready to take their first steps toward becoming future SFRPD team members. Through hands-on training, teamwork-based challenges, in camp practice, and guided mentorship, participants will build essential skills in communication, responsibility, problem-solving, and youth engagement. By the end of the program, each L.I.T. will walk away with a polished resume, a strong cover letter, a verification letter of community service hours, a certificate of completion, and a practical "Leader Tool Kit" filled with transition activities and group game ideas they can use when working in camps in the future. L.I.T. Pathways empower young leaders with the confidence, knowledge, and experience they need to support recreation programs and shine as positive role models in their communities. Filed trips are included in this camp.

classes.name: Girls Play Sports - Mission Rec - Summer Camp Ages 5-12
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Join us this summer for girls sports camp! Girls will have the opportunity to learn many different sports, like basketball, volleyball, soccer, tennis, softball, badminton and more. Girls will build confidence learning new skills in a fun and safe environment, and make new friends. There will be wonderful additions to the camp like cooking, art, and swimming. Come and join us for a fun, sports-filled camp! Drop off and pick up will be at Mission Arts Center at 747 Treat Ave.

classes.name: Everybody Plays - Summer Camp - Ages 6-12
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Everybody Plays is a specialized therapeutic day camp, designed for children with autism and other developmental disabilities. We strive to develop social, play, and life skills while fostering community integration and encouraging independence in a safe and nurturing environment. Your child will experience recreational therapy through expressive arts, sensory activities, collaborative games, nature, music, animal assisted therapy, community field trips and more! Campers can bring comfort items for coping, but please be mindful toys may be distracting. Please no electronics unless they are used for communication. This camp is for children with disabilities. Please pay close attention to the welcome letter for important details like different pick-up location. See pre-approval information at the beginning of this section. This camp does not offer extended care. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: Everybody Travels - Summer Camp - Ages 9-15
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 9 yrs but less than 16 yrs
classes.desc: Does your child love riding the SF Muni and taking long walks through the city? At Everybody Travels Camp, your child will have the opportunity to learn navigation skills with support from staff. Campers will meet onsite in the morning, explore the city during the day, and return to site in the afternoon. Because this is a traveling camp, campers and staff may return to site late due to public transportation delays. Campers must arrive onsite before the group leaves to avoid transportation issues and delays for the group. All transportation fees are included in the camp fee, but campers may be asked to bring money for some activities. Due to the nature of this camp, campers must be independent with personal care and have safety awareness skills to stay with the group in the community. Campers must have stamina to travel up those San Francisco hills and explore the city! This camp does not offer extended care. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: SLICE - Summer Camp - Ages 15-25
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 15 yrs but less than 26 yrs
classes.desc: SLICE is a community-focused program for teens and transition-aged youth with Autism or other developmental disabilities, ages 15–25. Designed to support growing independence, SLICE offers opportunities for young adults to engage in meaningful community service, develop professional and life skills, and participate in collaborative projects with other TR camps. Participants practice social skills, team building, and community integration through hands-on activities and local field trips, including those planned with the Everybody Travels Camp. Due to a low staff ratio, participants must be independent with personal care, able to stay with the group with verbal prompting, and prepared for walking and travel during outings. For more information, call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: Teen City - Summer Camp - Ages 13-23
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 13 yrs but less than 24 yrs
classes.desc: Teen City provides the opportunity campers with disabilities ages 13-22 to create friendships, experience new recreation activities and practice life skills- without parents! Camp provides both structured and free choice activities to allow teens to develop social skills and increase self esteem. Our community integration activities allow for teens to practice budgeting, community service and transportation skills with support from staff. Campers experience recreational therapy through creative expression, collaborative activities and more! Campers may be asked to bring money for community outings. Please no electronics unless they are used for communication. Pay pay close attention to the welcome letter for important details like different pick-up location. This camp does not offer extended care. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: Tennis - Winter  Break Camp
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: The Winter Break Tennis Camp will use the Maunupau Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System curriculum (MM-LAFTS). Campers will learn grips, stances, appropriate footwork, correct swing flight paths for volleys and groundstrokes, the art of the rally, fun games and drills, and match play in a progressive fun format. Students will also learn vocabulary, chess, and work on a tennis-related art project.

classes.name: Pine Lake - Summer Camp- Ages 6-11
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 5y 9m but less than 12 yrs
classes.desc: Pine Lake Day Camp is located in the west end of Stern Grove. Pine Lake is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, field trips, arts and crafts and archery. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s’mores.

classes.name: Randall Museum Art Science & Technology Day Camp
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: The Randall Museum offers a wide variety of fun and engaging summer day camp experiences with emphasis on hands-on-learning, curiosity and creativity. Each course will include two specific classes taught by Randall Instructors, and up to 2 hours per day of supervised play and social time within the larger group. Classes may include: Science & Nature, Theater, Arts & Crafts, Woodworking, Ceramics, Cooking, Tennis, and New Technologies. The specific topics for each Course# will be described in detail after March 2 at randallmuseum.org. Due to complex topics and multi-step nature of many of the projects, partial attendance is strongly discouraged. This camp does not provide extended care

classes.name: Shred N' Butter - Summer Camp- Ages 12-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: An Introduction to skateboarding for beginners and intermediate skaters. Learn the basics of skateboarding from balance, pushing, turning and cruising on low impact ramps and surfaces. We offer a safe place for skaters to learn how to stand on a skateboard properly while maintaining our balance. We will also offer instruction for intermediate skaters on transitional ramps. We will cover the safety aspects and “flow” of skating flat ground obstacles/ramps. Instructors will assist in basic skateboard maintenance and identify each part of the skateboard. Our instructors have extensive experience in teaching and helping youth navigate their way through the fundamentals of skateboarding.

classes.name: Goal-Getters  - Summer Camp- Ages 7-12
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 7 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Join us at Camp Goal Getter where our goal is to have a fun, safe and exciting summer! We will rotate between soccer, flag football and basketball throughout each session. In addition to these great sports, participants will have a chance to join in on fun arts and crafts projects, games and much more!

classes.name: Fog City Summer Camp - Ages 8-13
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 8:30 AM - 5:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 14 yrs
classes.desc: Looking for a fun and active summer experience for your child? Fog City Summer Camp offers a wide variety of engaging sports, games, and creative activities for all skill levels. Campers will participate in team-based games such as basketball, Capture the Flag, European handball, soccer, and dodgeball, all designed to build cooperation, confidence, and communication. In addition to team-based sports, campers will swim at Sava Pool, enjoy arts and crafts projects, and have daily journal writing opportunities. We encourage participants to come ready to be active, try new things, and work together. Please have your child wear comfortable clothing suitable for movement and closed-toe shoes.

classes.name: Girls PLAY Sports - 3-Day Volleyball Winter Camp
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 11 yrs
classes.desc: Join our 3-Day Girls Volleyball Camp to improve skills, build confidence, and have fun! Athletes will work on serving, passing, hitting, defense, and teamwork through drills, instruction, and game play in a positive and competitive environment.

classes.name: Girls PLAY Sports - 3-Day Volleyball Winter Camp
classes.ages: Age at least 11 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Join our 3-Day Girls Volleyball Camp to improve skills, build confidence, and have fun! Athletes will work on serving, passing, hitting, defense, and teamwork through drills, instruction, and game play in a positive and competitive environment.

classes.name: Upper Noe Sports - Summer Camp Ages 9-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 9 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Upper Noe recreation center is proud to present the Upper Noe summer sports day camp! Our multi-sport camp for children ages 9 to 14 provides a dynamic and engaging and supportive environment where campers participate in two different sports each day. This approach is designed to foster a well-rounded athletic experience, promote physical development, and teach valuable life skills like teamwork and sportspersonship, while participating in the following sports with our amazing professional staff: Volleyball -Tennis-Basketball-Flag Football-Kickball-Dodgeball-Soccer-Capture the flag and Hunters and gatherers just to name a few!

classes.name: City Outdoor Rec Expedition - Ages 12-14
classes.when: Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 12 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: CORE: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather. Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

classes.name: Ballroom Dance
classes.location: Betty Ann Ong Chinese Rec Center
classes.desc: Learn and improve your ballroom dance basic skills in a social setting. A dance partner is not required. Enjoy the music while getting in a bit of exercise! This is an instructional class. No drop-ins.

classes.name: Ballroom Dance
classes.location: Betty Ann Ong Chinese Rec Center
classes.desc: Learn and improve your ballroom dance basic skills in a social setting. A dance partner is not required. Enjoy the music while getting in a bit of exercise! This is an instructional class. No drop-ins.

classes.name: Beginner's Ballroom Dance Lesson
classes.desc: Get social in this senior beginner's ballroom dance program. There will be a beginner’s level instructional monthly dance theme such as waltz, cha cha, rhumba, disco, salsa, and more.

classes.name: Chinese Line Dancing
classes.desc: Being similar to the Western line dance, instructions (including proper demonstrations at the beginning of the class) will be given to the Chinese line dancers with dancing patterns (terminology) and step counts, and following with practices led by the instructor, except that much more emphasis will be placing on hands and arms, feet and whole body coordination, making whole body movement accents in relation to the Chinese music, expressing the character of various Chinese ethnic groups and being incorporated with modern dance. Currently it is also known as Fitness (Dancing) Exercise being very popular in China. Our class at GGPSC is of beginners/intermediate level.

classes.name: Line Dancing - Intermediate
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level line dance class. No partner needed to participate in this dance class. Class will involve some turning dance steps.

classes.name: Line Dancing - Intermediate
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level line dance class. No partner needed to participate in this dance class. Class will involve some turning dance steps.

classes.name: Line Dancing - Intermediate
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level line dance class. No partner needed to participate in this dance class. Class will involve some turning dance steps.

classes.name: Line Dancing - Ultra Beginners
classes.desc: Learn the basics of Line Dancing. You will learn steps, figures and patterns of line dances to all genres of music. No partners are needed. New and challenged seniors are encouraged. This course is an introduction for advanced dances. It is an ongoing class for beginners. No extra materials are needed except a comfortable pair of shoes for dancing.

classes.name: Line Dancing (Intermediate Level)
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level line dance class. No partner needed to participate in this dance class. Class will involve some turning dance steps.

classes.name: Line Dancing (Intermediate Level)
classes.desc: This is an intermediate level line dance class. No partner needed to participate in this dance class. Class will involve some turning dance steps.

classes.name: Argentine Tango - Beginning
classes.desc: Join this low impact tango class which welcomes all levels of dancers. Detailed instruction and partner participation. This class will incorporate a variety of styles that developed in different regions and eras.

classes.name: Drop-in: Zumba
classes.desc: Zumba is a dance-fitness program that combines Latin inspired dance moves with easy to follow choreography. It is designed to provide an energetic and fun aerobic workout. There is now a minimal fee for Indoor Zumba. Walk-up outdoor classes are free. Visit sfrecpark.org/zumba for a full list of current class information. Purchase a discounted multi-visit pass at sfrecpark.org/register under Passes & Memberships.

The intention is awareness at a physical, mental and emotional level on as well as off the mat.

classes.name: Garden Explorers Club
classes.ages: Age at least 5 yrs but less than 8 yrs
classes.desc: Do you love being outside? Are you curious about nature? Join us in the Randall's gardens where we'll explore and learn about the plants and animals that live there, plant and grow our own seeds in the greenhouse, play worm bin bingo, create nature-based crafts and more!

classes.name: Mad Scientists Mashup!
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 9 yrs
classes.desc: Mad Scientists unite! Experimentation, creativity and play bring scientific concepts to life in this hands-on class. We'll explore the physical sciences by building, mixing, blasting and experimenting with new science topics each week!

classes.name: New Technologies: 3D Printing and Laser Cutting Fab Lab
classes.desc: In this course, your imagination is the only limit to what you can create. Here you will learn to use free 3D modeling and vector drawing software to imagine and model your designs, then use the powerful creative tools of our lab to bring your one-of-a-kind parts, assemblies and artworks to life in all their glory. Both beginners and experienced students are welcome. This is a project-based class and consistent attendance is required. Those who miss the first 2 classes or who are excessively late or absent may be dropped without refund.

classes.name: New Technologies: Electronic Inventor Lab with AI Coding
classes.desc: What if you could invent the machine to feed your dog when it's hungry, or the clothing that lights up to the rhythms of your heart beat? In this hands-on course we’ll use AI to do the coding while we focus on learning to use programmable microcontrollers (Arduinos) to interface with the physical world through sensors, animating LED displays, motors, and more. Moving from group projects to individual inventions, you will learn the principles and theory of practical electronics and circuit building, as well as best practices for creating AI generated code that can enable your creative power. Learn how to get your ideas and inventions out of your head and into existence. No experience is required. Basic electronics supplies are provided for the class, but there’s an additional material fee of $40. A paid subscription to an AI service such as ChatGPT or Gemini is recommended. Repeat students are welcomed and can work on their ongoing projects. This class pairs well with a Digital Fab Lab course that focuses on building and fabrication.

Along the way, we'll learn about circuits, sensors & even a bit of programming. This is a project-based class and consistent attendance is required, those who miss the first 2 classes or who are excessively late or absent may be dropped without refund.

classes.name: New Technologies: Laser Cut Animals, Heroes, and More!
classes.ages: Age at least 10 yrs but less than 14 yrs
classes.desc: Do you have a favorite animal or superhero? A character that represents your creative self? Using simple line drawing software we’ll engrave and then laser cut these figures, shapes, and drawings from plywood. You’ll color, decorate, and learn to adapt your design to make stencils for painting, key rings, and more. This is a fun, easy way to gain experience with basic design software and the precision digital maker tools in the Fab Lab. This is a project-based class and consistent attendance is required, those who miss the first 2 classes or who are excessively late or absent may be dropped without refund.

Prebuilt robots will be progressively modified and adapted for games and challenges such as robot races, bumper cars, baseball, tag and soccer. classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 10 yrs
classes.desc: Do you like to figure out how things work? Do you enjoy building things? In this Fab Lab class at the Randall Museum, you'll design and build your own robot from scratch using scrap parts, motors, and common circuitry components. You'll learn about gears, motors, sensors, LEDs, how electricity works, and much more. We'll emphasize creativity, invention, problem solving, and teamwork. This is a project-based class and consistent attendance is required, those who miss the first 2 classes or who are excessively late or absent may be dropped without refund.

classes.name: Deaf Club
classes.desc: Come to a place where we build Deaf community, language and culture. The Deaf Club will be meeting once a month on the first Friday of each month during the season. This space provides time for Deaf community members and their families, as well as anyone interested in engaging in Deaf culture and developing American Sign Language skills. The first half of the evening is designed to support those learning American Sign Language (ASL), including ASL students, and parents who have a child who is Deaf or hard of hearing. The second half of the evening is focused on providing a space for members of the Deaf community, to share culture, resources and socialize in a sign language-only environment. Anyone is welcome to stay for the second half of the evening; but they should be prepared to hold their own in conversation. This is a drop-in program, but a Recreation and Parks Play Pass or sign-in is required.

classes.name: Disability Sports: Power Chair Soccer
classes.desc: Power Soccer is a disability sport, played in specially modified power wheelchairs. The Golden State Ducks SF Power Soccer program is presented by SF Recreation and Parks and Bay Area Outreach and Recreation Program (BORP), this program welcomes beginner and experienced players. Participants can enjoy practice play or high-level tournament competition! If you do not have your own power soccer chair, we may be able to provide a chair for you to use to help you get started. And if you want to keep playing, we can help connect you with resources for purchasing your own chair! After you register, or if you have questions or want to check out the sport before you register, reach out to goldenstateducks@gmail.com. Eureka Valley Recreation Center is located on 100 Collingwood St, San Francisco, CA 94114.

Weight requirement is between 40–300 lbs. Open Climb sessions are one hour long, and while enrollment is not required in advance, it is highly suggested. Fill out the enrollment form on the Glen Park Rec Center page at sfrecpark.org. Adults are welcome. Drop-ins permitted. Scholarships accepted.

classes.name: Rock Climbing 101
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Discover the thrill of rock climbing with Rock Climbing 101! In this beginner-friendly class, you'll learn essential skills and techniques, from properly securing your harness to mastering foundational climbing moves. By the end of the course, you'll feel confident tackling more challenging routes and climbing with friends. All climbing gear is provided. Participants must weigh between 40 and 300 lbs. and wear closed-toe shoes.
