classes.name: Archery - Halloween Tournament
classes.ages: Age at least 8 yrs but less than 18 yrs
classes.desc: Join us for a thrilling Halloween-themed archery tournament at the oldest public archery range in the USA! Test your skills by shooting at both 40cm and 60cm targets, as well as fun 3D targets. Adult distances are set at 18 meters, while youth distances are 9 meters. Compete in various bow categories, including: Olympic Recurve, Barebow Recurve, Traditional, Compound Unlimited, Compound Bowhunter, and Compound Barebow. Awards will be given after the event. Participants must bring their own equipment. Please contact joseph.arnaldo@sfgov.org for more information. Deadline to register, OCTOBER 16TH 2026.

classes.name: SF Adaptive Cycling Program
classes.desc: The San Francisco Adaptive Cycling Program is a collaboration between Recreation and Parks, MTA, and BORP Adaptive Sports and Recreation. The program runs Saturdays, 10PM -2PM, June-October in Golden Gate Park, behind the Bandshell in the Music Concourse. Reservations are required and bike availability is limited. To make a reservation: call 510-848-2930 or email cycling@borp.org. All bike reservations must be made before 5pm on the Thursday before the event. Include your Full Name, Bike Type requested (if known), and requested date and time of arrival in your reservation request.

classes.name: Girls PLAY Sports Hoop Summit
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: A full day of basketball fundamentals, skills, games, fun and more. Including a guest speaker! Bring a healthy lunch and water bottle.

classes.name: Tang Soo Doo - Intermediate
classes.desc: This class is for students that have achieved Orange Belt (7th Gup in Korean) and above. Conceptual knowledge is required. One should have already learned the basic skills needed for advancement. The Pyung Form signifies the changing aspect of Tang Soo Do, called the Turtle. The Turtle has "peace of mind." It's the slow steady controlled pace to success. Mind, Body, and Spirit.

classes.name: Tang Soo Doo House - All Skill Levels
classes.desc: This course is open to students of all skill levels, from complete beginners to experienced practitioners. Instruction emphasizes the development of strong fundamentals, proper technique, and overall physical fitness through structured training. Students will build confidence, learn practical self-defense skills, and have opportunities to advance at their own pace in a supportive and disciplined environment.

classes.name: Tang Soo Doo House - Beginning
classes.desc: Tang Soo Do is a traditional Korean martial art whose name reflects its roots: Tang (from the Tang Dynasty), Soo (open hand or way of the hand), and Do (way of life). This course introduces students to the philosophy and practice of Tang Soo Do, with training in kicking, punching, blocking, and other open-hand techniques. Instruction emphasizes discipline, physical conditioning, and self-defense skills. Students will also have the opportunity to purchase uniforms and belts for an additional fee.

classes.name: Play Day
classes.ages: Age at least 6 yrs but less than 13 yrs
classes.desc: Play Day is an opportunity for parents to drop off their children with Autism or other developmental disabilities for a play day with our trained staff. Then, parents can go have a play day themselves! Kids will enjoy learning new sports skills, cooperative games, cooking, community field trips, and more. You provide snack and lunch for your child and we provide the rest. Space is limited. For new participants, one of our Therapeutic Recreation Coordinators will contact you for a recreation assessment after you register. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: Teen Time
classes.ages: Age at least 13 yrs but less than 23 yrs
classes.desc: For teens with Autism or other developmental disabilities, ages 13-22. Teens will focus on social skills, life skills, cooperative games, community field trips and more. This is an opportunity for parents to drop off their teens for some Teen Time with our trained staff. Then, parents can go have some time for themselves! You provide snack and lunch for your teen and we provide the rest. For new participants, one of our Therapeutic Recreation Coordinators will contact you for a recreation assessment after you register. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: TR Social Club
classes.ages: Age at least 14 yrs but less than 25 yrs
classes.desc: For teens and transition-aged youth with Autism or other developmental disabilities, ages 14-25. This is an opportunity for teens and young adults to have time with peers--without parents! We strive to develop social and life skills while fostering community integration and encouraging independence in a safe and nurturing environment. Due to the low staff ratio, participants should be independent with personal care and able to stay with the group with verbal/visual prompting. For more information call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

classes.name: Basketball - Girls MRC Rebels Tryouts Fall 2026
classes.when: Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri · 5:30 PM - 7:30 PM
classes.ages: Age at least 9 yrs but less than 15 yrs
classes.desc: Come tryout for the MRC Rebels AAU girls basketball program. For more information contact Chibuzo Emeka at chibuzo.emeka@sfgov.org.

classes.name: ACCESS To Health & Fitness
classes.ages: Age at least 18 yrs but less than 26 yrs
classes.desc: Come get healthy and fit! This is a specialized Therapeutic Recreation Program, serving transition aged youth with disabilities. We strive to develop social and life skills while fostering community integration and encouraging independence in a safe and nurturing environment. Therapeutic Recreation Staff will work directly with the ACCESS students, and transition age adults to help them develop healthy lifestyle and fitness skills.

classes.name: Drop-in: Girls Bay Sox Specialty Clinic
classes.ages: Age at least 11 yrs but less than 17 yrs
classes.desc: Whether you are looking to dominate on the mound, command the field from behind the dish, or build the explosive power needed to level up your entire game, these focused, high-energy sessions are built for you. Led by experienced Bay Sox coaches and guest instructors, our clinics offer elite, position-specific training in a supportive, competitive environment of your peers.

classes.name: TR Family Day
classes.desc: Join us for some family fun and connection! This program is for families and friends of our Therapeutic Recreation programs. Open to siblings, caregivers, and others, our TR Family Days program provides the opportunity for families of children with disabilities to connect with one another, create friendships, experience new recreation activities, and build community. Come join us for our family outings this season. Note that at least one caregiver must accompany your child for this program. All family members attending must be registered and must plan to meet us at the designated location/outing. Outings will be announced by email two weeks prior to the activity. Families who are registered will receive the meeting location for the outing. Get on the list to hear about upcoming outings by calling 415-831-6841 or emailing us at rpdtr@sfgov.org.
